title: Operations Executive (24/7 Shift)
topic_label: trading operations, trading business, commodities trading
best_module: IS4303 – IT-mediated financial solutions and platforms
description: About Us AsiaNext is an institution-only, global exchange for digital assets. A first-of-its-kind, combining the innovation of crypto with the robust security of traditional finance, we offer a comprehensive platform for trading and tokenising a diverse range of securities and non-bankable assets. Our services include listing, trading, clearing, settlement, and custody. A joint venture between SBI Digital Asset Holdings Co., Ltd. (SBI DAH) and SIX Group AG (SIX), AsiaNext is built to meet the growing demand from institutional investors for a safe venue to trade digital assets. Where applicable, our business activities will be subject to the strict regulatory oversight of the Monetary Authority of Singapore, and we adhere to the highest standards of corporate governance and internal controls across all activities. Leveraging the extensive networks of SBI DAH and SDX between Asia and Europe, as well as their joint expertise, we have built AsiaNext since 2020. Our vision is to become a trusted, market-leading exchange whose members can safely trade a wide variety of digital assets, permitting greater institutional access and driving the new era of digital finance worldwide. Why Join Us? With SIX, the Swiss stock exchange, and Japan’s finance conglomerate SBI DAH as joint shareholders, AsiaNext has significant financial backing. The team at AsiaNext are seasoned professionals from the finance industry and are passionate about digital assets, innovation and excellence. We listen to one another; we learn together, and we collaborate with a diverse ecosystem. Join AsiaNext and be part of a dynamic, fast-growing team with opportunities for professional growth and development. You'll have the chance to work on cutting-edge projects that are shaping the future of finance About This Role: AsiaNext seeks to develop a fair, orderly and transparent exchange that facilitates members and participants to have equal access to market information and transparent trading rules. This role will be performed on shift work basis where the candidate will be primarily tasked to monitor the continuous smooth running of the platform processes, hourly settlement run, and work on the market surveillance role in monitoring, identifying and escalating instances of market abuse or misconduct and other manipulative practices that compromise the fair, orderly and transparent exchange operations of the trading platform. Responsibilities Act as the first line of client support on Member’s account or trade related queries. Monitor platform processes and ensure the system batch run is executed successfully for the hourly settlement run covering critical process such as MTM margin calculation and risk limit notification. Activate contingent procedure to mitigate any system or data feed outage that affect the system batch run and hourly margin computation. Monitor member’s cash balance and margin limit position and follow up with Members proactively to manage their margin call if it is triggered. Monitor member’s credit or concentration limit and follow up with Members to manage their risk limit exposure if it is breached. Escalation to Ops management on any major operation process, system run or data feed failure for coordinated and prompt resolution. Perform level 1 check on market surveillance alerts triggered and forward to Ops Management for level 2 review on alert closure or further escalation if the alert event affects the working of a fair, efficient and transparent market. Use Python, R, and SQL to extract data, perform simple analysis, and generate a basic report. Able to use MIS/monitoring tools such as PowerBi, Zabbix, Graffana etc. Prior experience of conducting user acceptance testing (either manual or automated) Qualifications And Experience Education Diploma in banking and finance, business management/Higher NITEC in business. Experience and Skill Prior experience working in a related role in the banking/securities services/fintech/payment industry will be desirable. Good knowledge of relevant regulatory and market conduct rules and other related regulations will be preferred. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills Proven analytical and eye for details Collaborative, self-discipline, and team focus Ability to pick up the learning with the training provided and work in a fast-paced environment Willing to work in different shifts to cover the 24x7 operations Rotate on fortnightly basis :- Time: 7.30am to 7.30pm (Day Shift) 7.30pm to 7.30am (Night Shift) Employment Benefits Competitive staff benefits - annual leave, life insurance, and medical insurance (includes dependents)

Analytical Skills, Quantitative Modeling, Market Analysis, Data Management, Risk Management, Problem Solving, Communication, Collaboration, Technical Proficiency (Excel / Python / SQL). Able to liaise confidently with both internal teams and clients. Problem-Solver: Resourceful, solutions-driven, and able to anticipate challenges before they arise. Opportunities to grow your career with real ownership and impact.

title: Head of Marketing, Singapore
description: IG Singapore is looking for a Head of Marketing, becoming a permanent member of our team. Working in the heart of the CBD on Raffles Place, you will be joining a team of passionate individuals seeking to grow our Singapore business and provide exceptional client service to our clients. So, who are we? Hello, we’re IG Group. No, not Instagram – though we're a pretty big deal ourselves. We’re a global, FTSE 250-listed company made up of a collection of progressive fintech brands in the world of online trading and investing. The best part? We’ve snapped up many awards for our top-class platforms, forward-thinking products, and incredible employee experiences. We believe in financial freedom. And our purpose is to help ambitious people achieve it. Around 400,000 people use our groundbreaking technology and forward-thinking platforms to trade the financial products they know and love. Did we mention we have offices in 18 countries? Yes, when you join IG Group, you'll have the chance to work across multiple brands, with people all over the world. Join us for an exciting future and let’s innovate together! Your role in the team We are seeking a strategic and results-driven Head of Marketing to lead our marketing efforts. Reporting directly to the CEO, this role will oversee a team and be responsible for driving the demanding growth of our new brokerage product while continuing to scale the existing CFD business. This is a key leadership position that requires both strategic vision and hands-on execution. You will own all marketing channels—digital, affiliates, social, PR, partnerships, and offline—and partner closely with sales, client servicing, and product teams to deliver measurable growth. The role is critical in shaping our regional brand presence, client acquisition, and engagement strategies, while ensuring marketing initiatives are compliant, data-driven, and aligned with business priorities. What you’ll do Lead and oversee all marketing channels, ensuring strategies are tailored to local market dynamics while aligned with global brand positioning. Manage the budget for markets and being responsible for cost effectiveness, resource allocation and measurable ROI. Drive the demanding growth of IG’s new brokerage product, while continuing to scale the existing CFD business. Partner closely with regional teams, performance team, sales, client servicing,  and product teams to ensure marketing initiatives directly support business objectives and client needs. Develop and execute integrated marketing strategies to drive awareness, acquisition, engagement, and retention across digital, performance, social, partnerships, PR, and offline channels. Manage agencies on record for communications, creative development and media buys. Continuously  track market trends and competitor activity to adapt strategies, seize  opportunities, and mitigate risks. Oversee  and inspire a team of marketers, building a high-performance, innovative, and collaborative culture. Uphold  regulatory and brand compliance standards across all campaigns and communications. Who we’re looking for You’re curious about things like the client experience, the rapid developments in tech, and the complex world of fintech regulation. You’re also a confident, creative thinker with a knack for innovating. We know that you know every problem has a solution. Here, you can try new ideas, and lead the way in creating inspiring experiences for our clients and everyone around you. We don’t fit the corporate stereotype. If you want to work for a traditional, suit-and-tie corporate that just gives you a pay cheque at the end of the month, we might not be for you. But, if you have that IG Group energy and you can stand behind what we believe in, let’s raise the bar together. What you’ll need for this role Minimum 10 years in tech or finance marketing, across traditional, social and digital channels. Minimum 3 years of equivalent leadership experience managing a team and a budget of US>$10M. Degree in marketing, business or finance. Strong understanding of investment & trading products (CFDs, FX, Crypto, Stocks) as well as an interest in the financial markets. Familiar with Gen AI tools and CRM systems such as Braze & Salesforce. Strategic mindset with a bias for action. Strong analytical skills, with a demonstrated ability to optimise and pivot on marketing plans for maximum effectiveness. Regional experience in Malaysia, HongKong, Taiwan, Thailand Fluency in English is a requirement, proficiency in Mandarin or Bahasa is a significant advantage to engage customers from Chinese/Bahasa native countries. How you’ll grow When you join IG Group, we want you to have more than a job – we want you to have a career. And you can. If you spot an opportunity, we want you to chase it. Stretch yourself, challenge your self-beliefs and go for the things you dream of. With internal and external learning opportunities, and the tools to help you skyrocket to success, we’ll support you all the way. And these opportunities truly are endless because we have some bold targets. We plan to expand our global presence, increase revenue growth, and ultimately deliver the world’s best trading experience. We’d love to have you along for the ride. The perks It really is more than a job. We’ll recognise your talent and make sure that you can still have a life – at work, and outside of it. Networks, committees, awards, sports and social clubs, mentorships, volunteering opportunities, extra time off… the list goes on. Performance-related bonuses Insurance and medical plans Career-focused technical and leadership training Two volunteering days per year One day of birthday leave per year Contribution to gym memberships and more Flexible working hours and work-from–home opportunities Where you’ll work We follow a hybrid working model; we reckon it’s the best of both worlds. This model also feeds into our secret ingredients for innovation: diversity, flexibility, and close connection. Plus, you’ll be welcomed into a diverse and inclusive workforce with a lot of creative energy. Ask our employees what their favourite thing is about working at IG, and you’ll hear an echo of ‘our culture’! That’s because you can come to work as your authentic self. The things that make you, you – like your ethnicity, sexual orientation, faith, age, gender identity/expression or physical capacity – can bring a fresh perspective or new skill to our business. That’s why we welcome people from various walks of life; and anyone who wants to help us realise our vision and strategy. So, if you’re keen to connect with our values, and lead the charge on innovation, you know what to do. Apply now

title: Senior Associate, Voice of Brand
description: About KK39 KK39 is the investment arm of Wee Hur Holdings Ltd., a Singapore-listed group with a long-standing reputation in real estate in Singapore and Australia. We are evolving into a professionalised multi-asset investment platform spanning Private Equity, Private Credit, and Venture Capital, and are actively strengthening our industry presence and relationships with LPs, GPs, and ecosystem partners. To support this growth, KK39 is seeking a Voice of Brand (Senior Associate) who will spearhead our branding, communications, and engagement efforts to ensure KK39’s presence in the investment community is visible, credible, and differentiated. Role Overview The Voice of Brand (Senior Associate) will act as KK39’s branding and communications lead, responsible for building a consistent external voice for the firm. This role blends strategic communications, event curation, and stakeholder engagement to elevate KK39’s profile in the investment ecosystem. You will work closely with the investment team, translating KK39’s activities, perspectives, and initiatives into compelling narratives and touchpoints that resonate with LPs, GPs, and partners. Key Responsibilities 1. Brand Strategy & Positioning · Develop and refine KK39’s brand narrative to reflect our unique positioning as a listed-group-backed investment office. · Ensure consistency of tone, voice, and design across all communication channels. · Build branding frameworks (templates, messaging guides, outreach playbooks) that the investment team can easily adopt. 2. Events & Community Engagement · Lead the end-to-end planning, coordination, and execution of KK39-hosted events (eg. LP/GP networking sessions, roundtables, thought-leadership dinners). · Manage event logistics, invitations, attendee engagement, and follow-up communications. · Build and maintain a calendar of industry events and conferences; coordinate KK39’s participation, visibility, and speaking opportunities. 3. Content & Communications · Craft thought-leadership content (LinkedIn posts, press releases, newsletters, and op-eds) to highlight KK39’s activities and perspectives. · Produce post-event summaries and follow-up campaigns to ensure engagement is maximised beyond the event itself. · Coordinate with external partners (media, PR, design vendors) as needed. 4. Stakeholder Engagement · Work alongside the investment team to strengthen relationships with LPs, GPs, and ecosystem partners by creating professional and timely touchpoints. · Track attendee feedback, engagement data, and industry response to measure brand-building effectiveness. · Support development of investor-facing materials (event decks, thought pieces, conference presence). 5. Internal Enablement · Free up the investment team by owning the operational and execution-heavy aspects of branding and events. · Maintain an internal repository of content, event templates, and standard communications for reuse. Job Requirements · Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or related field. · 3–5 years of experience in branding, corporate communications, or marketing within financial services, investment firms, or professional services. · Strong understanding of brand strategy, positioning, and messaging. · Excellent written and verbal communication skills; ability to craft compelling narratives. · Familiarity with content creation for LinkedIn, press releases, newsletters, and thought-leadership pieces. · Ability to manage external vendors (PR agencies, media, design). · Strong organizational skills and attention to detail. · Proficiency in digital marketing tools and social media platforms. · Comfortable working in a fast-paced, multi-asset investment environment.

title: Studio Technical Support Crew
description:
AP Media is a creative media tech company that represents a new multi-disciplinary intersection point in the industry. At a time when audiences are craving for more immersive digital content, we are here to provide the technical expertise, experimental thinking and marketing innovation brands need to thrive. We aim to make futurealistic experiences across a spectrum of domains, from real-time 3D set mapping, virtual and hybrid activations to interactive live streaming, immersive multimedia and experiential marketing. We want to make these experiences accessible to everyone. To do that, we help connect the dots across six key dimensions for new brand opportunities you can create today. We are looking for an open-minded, proactive, resourceful and dynamic individual, to join as a Virtual Production Camera Technician Job Description: • Set up, configure, operate, and perform
preventive maintenance on all studio and virtual production equipment,
including cameras, LED/VP processing units, tracking systems, video routers,
and AV infrastructure, ensuring every system is production-ready and
functioning reliably. • Troubleshoot camera and imaging
equipment and provide clear technical reports to the Studio Manager and
manufacturers to support timely issue resolution and minimise downtime. • Maintain system performance by updating
firmware and software across all devices and ensuring compatibility with
real-time production workflows. • Ensure that all technical equipment
meets operational and safety standards, with minimal disruption to studio
activities and production schedules. • Read, interpret, and design camera and
signal-flow diagrams, supporting clear and efficient routing for both physical
and virtual production environments. • Manage and route video signals,
including split camera feeds, genlock, timecode, and data lines, ensuring
synchronised and stable connections during shoots. • Apply strong knowledge of professional
video standards, including HD-SDI, DisplayPort, HDMI, RAW workflows, colour
metadata, and data-wrangling practices used in virtual production and broadcast
pipelines. • Maintain and support Virtual Production
systems, including tracking objects, markers, cabling, power management, and
full AV signal flow across the LED volume. • Assist the camera crew with calibration
workflows, including lens calibration, spatial calibration, and mounting of
virtual production tracking hardware on set. • Troubleshoot video feeds, tracking
stability, and calibration issues in collaboration with the Virtual Production
team to maintain accurate real-time rendering and scene playback. • Communicate changeover updates, camera
configuration changes, and system status to the Virtual Production team to
ensure smooth transitions and operational clarity. • Continuously develop skills and stay
updated on new technologies, maintaining a forward-thinking and adaptable
approach to evolving industry tools and workflows. • Proficient in video editing, supporting
quick reviews, technical tests, and content preparation when required.

title: Senior Executive, Marketing & Consumer Insights (Fragrance)

With a strong regional presence in Southeast Asia, their teams collaborate across borders to deliver innovative solutions for the world's most recognized FMCG brands. They pride ourselves on blending scientific precision with creative storytelling to stay ahead of market trends. about the job As a Senior Marketing Executive, you will play a dual-impact role, acting as the bridge between regional market intelligence and commercial execution. Minimum 3-5 years of marketing experience, ideally within B2B fragrance, B2B flavour, ingredients. title: Public Relations & Investor Relations Senior Executive
description: Primech Holdings is seeking a strategic, resourceful, and forward-thinking Senior Executive, Public Relations, Communications & Investor Relations to strengthen the company’s reputation, credibility, and visibility among key stakeholders. This role plays a critical part in shaping Primech’s corporate narrative, driving media engagement, supporting investor communications, and producing high-impact strategic materials. The ideal candidate is an exceptional storyteller with strong public relations foundations, excellent writing skills, and the ability to support cross-functional leadership on high-priority communication and investor-related projects. Key Responsibilities: 1. Public Relations & Media Engagement Develop and execute public relations strategies that align with business objectives, product milestones, and corporate initiatives. Build and maintain strong relationships with journalists, media outlets, and industry partners. Draft and distribute press releases, statements, feature articles, and other corporate communication materials. Coordinate and manage media interviews, press conferences, and publicity opportunities. Track, analyse, and report media coverage; provide insights and recommendations to leadership. 2. Corporate & Executive Communications Create speeches, talking points, media advisories, and key communication materials for senior management. Lead media pitching, media engagement efforts, and executive interview preparations. Monitor industry developments, public sentiment, and relevant regulatory updates to ensure timely and strategic communication responses. 3. Social Media & Content Development Oversee the strategy and content direction for corporate social media platforms (Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, LinkedIn). Manage editorial calendars, campaigns, and storytelling initiatives aligned with brand objectives. Produce compelling and on-brand content that strengthens engagement and supports public relations and IR campaigns. 4. Campaigns, Events & Public Relations Initiatives Plan and execute integrated campaigns, public relations activations, and partnership initiatives. Oversee branding, messaging, and creative assets for events, exhibitions, investor engagements, and key presentations. Manage event-related media interactions, including press briefings, interviews, and promotional activities. Work closely with internal teams and external vendors to deliver high-impact outcomes. 5. Investor Relations (IR) & Strategic Materials Support Assist Senior Management in developing and refining investor-facing materials, including investor presentations, quarterly updates, corporate decks, fact sheets, and strategic communication assets. Conduct market, competitor, and industry research to support investor insights and positioning. Contribute to investor-related marketing materials and storytelling, ensuring clarity, accuracy, and alignment with corporate messaging. Support the development of pitch decks, fundraising materials, and strategic proposals for leadership, especially for high-priority assignments from our CEO. Ensure consistency and cohesion between public and invesotr relations narratives to strengthen the company’s strategic communication framework. 6. Cross-Functional & Ad-Hoc Strategic Support Collaborate with cross-functional teams on corporate initiatives requiring communications or IR expertise. Provide flexible, on-demand support for strategic projects and presentation materials as requested by our CEO. Prepare slides, conduct research, draft content, and support strategic communication planning for internal and external stakeholders. 7. Research, Insights & Reporting Conduct market and competitor research to identify trends, risks, and communication opportunities. Monitor media coverage, investor sentiment, and broader industry conversations related to the company and its stakeholders. Track and report on the effectiveness of public relations, communications, and IR activities to guide continuous improvement. Minimum Requirements: Degree in Marketing, Communications, Public Relations, or a related field. Minimum 2 years of relevant experience in public relations, communications, or marketing, ideally in a fast-paced or corporate environment. Strong skills in corporate storytelling, brand messaging, and strategic communication. Proven ability to develop content across multiple formats (press releases, speeches, social media, executive decks, corporate publications). Excellent project management skills with strong attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple priorities effectively. Proficiency with presentation and design tools (e.g., PowerPoint, Canva). Collaborative, resourceful, and adaptable, with the ability to support cross-functional stakeholders and ad-hoc projects.

title: Integrated Social Lead, APAC + SG
description: About WPP Media WPP is the creative transformation company. We use the power of creativity to build better futures for our people, planet, clients and communities. For more information, visit wpp.com. WPP Media is WPP’s global media collective. In a world where media is everywhere and in everything, we bring the best platform, people, and partners together to create limitless opportunities for growth. For more information, visit wppmedia.com At WPP Media, we believe in the power of our culture and our people. It’s what elevates us to deliver exceptional experiences for both our clients and each other. In this role it will be critical to embrace WPP & WPP Media’s shared core values: Be Extraordinary by Leading Collectively to Inspire transformational Creativity. Create an Open environment by Balancing People and Client Experiences by Cultivating Trust . Lead Optimistically by Championing Growth and Development to Mobilize the Enterprise . Role Summary and Impact The Integrated Social Lead will define, champion, and evolve WPP Media's market-leading paid social media strategy and capabilities across the diverse markets of the Asia Pacific region. This role is tasked with building an industry-leading paid social practice that delivers media transformation for clients by leveraging advanced tactics across all relevant platforms. The mission is to drive superior media performance, seamlessly integrate paid social with broader media planning and buying, set regional standards of excellence, and achieve measurable business outcomes. The Integrated Social Lead, APAC, will be a visionary architect, shaping and leading WPP Media's paid social discipline across the dynamic and diverse markets of the APAC region, with particular attention to Singapore Local and Regional Clients. This role demands a unique blend of strategic foresight, commercial acumen, creative innovation, and analytical prowess, with a deep understanding of how to optimize paid social strategies within holistic media plans across all social platforms . This leader will define regional best practices for paid social, foster collaboration across WPP Media agencies and client teams, and drive cutting-edge solutions. They will mobilize the enterprise to deliver unparalleled paid social expertise, leveraging WPP Media's proprietary technologies, industry-leading platform accreditations, and scaled partnerships to build better futures for our clients and communities in APAC + SG. Skills and Experience 1. Paid Social Strategy & Leadership Define and lead WPP Media's overarching paid social media strategy for the APAC region, aligning with media transformation and client success across all social platforms within the regional media context. Champion a unified paid social practice framework, integrating planning and buying into holistic media strategies, while developing advanced first-party data strategies for enhanced targeting and measurement. Identify, integrate, and operationalize emerging paid social trends, platform innovations, and strategic opportunities (e.g., AI in paid social, advanced programmatic buying, new media products). Guide performance creative strategies, leveraging DCO and AI-driven creative, and utilize automation and machine learning for predictive campaign optimization and efficient activation across paid social channels. 2. Client Engagement & Thought Leadership Act as a senior strategic advisor for WPP Media's top-tier clients within the APAC region, positioning the organization as the authoritative voice in paid social strategy, execution, and media investment across all social platforms . Cultivate trust and expand long-term relationships by providing visionary insights on cross-platform social media allocation, budgeting, and performance, driving new business and shaping regional market narratives. 3. Performance Measurement & Optimization Establish and drive advanced measurement methodologies, including incrementality testing, multi-touch attribution, and unified media measurement models, for paid social media effectiveness and ROI across various client portfolios. Lead actionable analytics and A/B testing to continuously optimize paid social strategies and campaigns, enhancing engagement, audience growth, and demonstrable business outcomes across all regional markets. 4. Governance, Standards & Platform Alliances Establish and uphold regional standards for paid social media excellence, compliance, and ethical engagement across WPP Media's network, considering local regulations and media buying guidelines. Lead strategic alliances with all top social platforms and significant regional platforms, fostering innovation, competitive advantage, and access to beta features, particularly concerning media products, data, and measurement capabilities . 5. Collaboration & Talent Development Lead collectively by fostering strong collaboration and knowledge sharing across WPP Media's diverse agencies and expert communities (creative, strategy, data, media buying) within APAC + SG for paid social initiatives. Mentor, inspire, and develop high-performance paid social media professionals across the APAC + SG region, fostering continuous learning and growth with an emphasis on integrated social and media expertise. Qualifications Experience: 10-12+ years progressive experience in paid social media leadership within a large media agency network, global media buying entity, or media-focused global brand. This role is ideal for someone who thrives on creating engaging digital content, with a particular emphasis on photo and video production. You'll have the unique opportunity to connect with our beneficiaries, partners and volunteers, capture their stories, and bring them to life in a way that resonates deeply with our audience, all while upholding our brand guidelines and narrative principles. (e.g. TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube). Proactively identify and develop compelling stories from our beneficiaries, volunteers, and staff that complements visual assets and reinforces brand messaging. Assist in managing a content calendar to ensure timely and relevant posting across all channels. Conduct sensitive and empathetic interviews with beneficiaries, ensuring their stories are told respectfully and authentically, adhering to all privacy, consent and Habitat Singapore’s safeguarding guidelines. Translate complex experiences into relatable and impactful narratives that inspire action and support. Actively showcase diverse activities and positive experience of our volunteers. Develop campaigns that encourage volunteer recruitment and retention. B) Brand Guardianship: Ensure all communications and marketing materials adhere strictly to brand guidelines, tone, and narrative. Maintain a consistent and professional brand image across all platforms and touchpoints. Meticulously protect our brand's integrity and strategic interests across all touchpoints. This includes ensuring consistent and appropriate brand representation with our beneficiaries, in collaborations with corporate partners, and throughout all internal and external communications from the charity itself. C) General Marketing Support: Assist with other marketing and communications initiatives as required, including documents and presentations, email marketing, website updates, and public relations efforts. Collaborate with internal teams to ensure consistent brand messaging. Assist in developing content for fundraising projects and campaigns. Provide support for collaborative projects with stakeholders and partners. A successful candidate would be · A diploma or tertiary graduate holding qualifications in Mass Communications, Marketing, Journalism, Multimedia Arts, or a related field. · Minimum experience of two years in communications, marketing, or a social media role, with a portfolio showcasing video content production. · Demonstrates expertise in creating engaging short-form video content for social media platforms (Facebook, TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube). · Proficiency in video editing software (Adobe Premiere Pro, Final. Cut Pro, CapCut) and graphic design tools (Adobe Photoshop, Canva). · Strong written and verbal communication skills, with a keen eye for detail and good copywriting abilities. · Ability to conduct sensitive interviews and extract compelling stories from individuals, particularly those from diverse backgrounds. · Good command of English and able to speak a second language (Mandarin, Malay, Tamil) and/or local dialects to communicate with. · Good understanding of social media trends, best practices, and analytics. · Ability to work independently and as part of a team · A passion for our charity's mission and a genuine desire to make a positive impact. · Comfortable to uphold, communicate and affirm Habitat for Humanity’s principles (vision and mission); Pls refer to https://www.habitat.org.sg/about-us for details. If you are interested to join us and be part of our exciting and meaningful ministry, please email us your complete curriculum vitae . Do include a cover letter with your salary expectations and email it to hr@habitat.org.sg . Only short-listed candidates will be contacted for an interview. Habitat's Expectations Surrounding Safeguarding: Habitat for Humanity requires that all employees take seriously their ethical responsibilities to safeguarding our intended beneficiaries, their communities, and all those with whom we work. Managers at all levels have particular responsibilities to support and develop systems that create and maintain an environment that prevents harassment, sexual exploitation and abuse; safeguards the rights of beneficiaries and community members (especially children); and promotes the implementation of Habitat for Humanity's code of conduct.

description: Risk Manager (Commodity Trading) Singapore | Industry: Multi-Commodities / Global Trading About Us We are a leading global Agri commodity trading and processing company headquartered in Singapore, connecting producers and consumers across more than 20 countries. We trade a diverse portfolio including grains, pulses, edible oils, cotton, and food ingredients, supported by an integrated supply chain and a strong commitment to sustainability and innovation. This role is ideal for a professional with deep expertise in market and credit risk who thrives in a fast-paced global trading environment. What You’ll Be Doing As Risk Manager , you will be responsible for overseeing market, credit, and operational risk across Agrocorp’s trading activities, ensuring robust risk controls, data integrity, and actionable insights for decision-making. Market & Credit Risk Management • Oversee daily P&L, mark-to-market (MTM), and exposure reporting for trading desks. • Validate data accuracy and ensure timely escalation of breaches or anomalies. • Monitor trading positions and exposures across physical and paper portfolios. • Conduct Value-at-Risk (VaR), stress testing, and scenario analyses to quantify exposures. • Set, review, and maintain trading and counterparty limits in line with risk appetite. Governance & Reporting • Prepare daily and periodic risk reports for senior management and traders. • Support credit reviews, counterparty assessments, and KYC onboarding. • Provide clear risk commentary and recommendations for decision-making. • Ensure compliance with internal risk frameworks and external regulatory standards. Who We’re Looking For • Experience: 5–12 years in risk management within a commodity trading or energy trading environment. • Technical Skills: Strong command of P&L explain, VaR, stress testing, and exposure analysis. • Tools: Advanced Excel (macros), proficiency in Python (required), and experience with CTRM systems. • Knowledge: Understanding of physical and derivatives markets, and trading lifecycle. • Mindset: Analytical, detail-oriented, and able to communicate complex ideas simply and clearly. • Education: Degree in Finance, Economics, Engineering, or a related quantitative discipline. Why Join Us • Be part of a growing, global trading organization headquartered in Singapore. • Play a key role in strengthening our global risk management framework . • Gain exposure to diverse commodities and global trading strategies. • Opportunity to lead risk analytics, automation, and system enhancements . • Competitive compensation, strong team culture, and professional development opportunities. description: Nanyang Business School (NBS) invites applications for the position of Senior Research Fellow (Post-doc) with Prof. Xin Chang’s research group. This position is supported by a Tier III grant to establish a Climate Transformation Programme (CTP) at NTU. As part of this programme, the Climate Finance Cluster will assess the multifaceted business risks posed by the physical impacts of climate change, evaluate the implications of carbon pricing and public procurement, and facilitate the transition to a low-carbon economy. The Senior Research Fellow will be responsible for developing integrated approaches to assess company-level climate risks, assembling datasets on mandatory carbon pricing and government procurement, and incorporating these resources into empirical analyses of financial markets and economic outcomes. The researcher will contribute to advancing how climate risks are measured, priced, and managed by companies and investors, and will see this work through to publication in major research journals. Duties and Responsibilities: Lead the development of company-level climate risk measures and related analytical frameworks, including the construction of databases, regional climate-risk summaries, and empirical models that incorporate climate risk into asset-pricing applications. Quantify the economic and financial impacts of mandatory carbon pricing and government procurement policies on firms and markets. Perform data management, large-scale statistical analyses, and ensure high standards of documentation and reproducibility. Work effectively with colleagues across the CTP and with local and international collaborators. Mentor junior colleagues and students. Write, present, and publish research findings in peer-reviewed journals. Knowledge and Experience Requirements: PhD degree in statistics, computer sciences, economics, or a closely related field. Excellent knowledge of computational linguistic tools and machine learning techniques. Experience with data analysis with Python and STATA. Prior experience in publishing in peer-reviewed business, economic, or scientific journals Excellent oral and written communication skills in English. Key Competency Requirements: Ability to work independently to develop research in challenging environments. Collaborative, with an interest in working with other researchers and scientists (this is a large team project) to produce high-quality and impactful research. Interest in climate, green, or sustainable finance. Excellent oral and written communication skills. Interested applicants are invited to submit a cover letter, full CV and names and contact details of three referees in one document. Enquiries about this position can be made to Nur Masturah (nurmasturah.am@ntu.edu.sg). We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Key Responsibilities Plan, shoot, and edit photos and short-form video content for Instagram, TikTok, and other social platforms Capture on-site content during events, trainings, prep, service, and behind-the-scenes moments Support day-to-day social media content execution in line with brand guidelines and campaign calendars Adapt key messages, narratives, and moments into engaging social formats Assist with posting, scheduling, and basic community engagement where required Work closely with marketing team colleagues to ensure visual and tonal consistency across brands Collaborate with the wider marketing team to support launches, activations, and partnerships Organise and maintain a structured library of video and photo assets for future use What We’re Looking For Experience in content creation, social media, or digital storytelling Strong interest in hospitality, food, drinks, culture, or lifestyle brands Comfortable shooting photos and videos on mobile, DJI, and DSLR/mirrorless cameras Confident with basic photo and video editing tools (e.g. Adobe Lightroom, CapCut, Premiere Pro or similar) Strong sense of timing, pacing, and storytelling for short-form video Able to work independently on shoots while collaborating closely with a wider team

title: Executive / Senior Executive / Assistant Manager / Manager (Comms) – Media Relations
description: About the Strategic Communications Unit (SCU) The Strategic Communications Unit (SCU) is responsible for creating, implementing and delivering timely, clear, concise, and effective messaging and communication strategies to enhance the corporate profile of SNEF and promulgate SNEF’s key messages across all media platforms. The SCU produces and disseminates information, messages and publications to relevant stakeholder groups through internal and external communication and works to raise the profile of SNEF internally and externally through the development of communication and media strategies that promote the mission of SNEF. The SCU will reside as a dedicated unit under the Policy Division helmed by the Head (Strategic Communications). Responsibilities: Media relations specialist Develop integrated communication plans for SNEF’s events and announcements Draft, edit and distribute communications materials such as speeches, press releases, talking points and information sheets that promotes the effective communication of SNEF’s messages and programmes to enhance the understanding of SNEF’s work and mission Pitch stories, manage media queries and interview requests Support the coordination and implementation of crisis comms strategy Monitor, analysis and prepare report on the impact of communications activities, reports on developments, trends and attitudes on issues of concern to SNEF Build and leverage on existing media relationships, as well as cultivate new contacts with mainstream, online, business and industry media partners (account manager for IWR) Job Requirements: Proficient in media communications, public relations or a related field (essential) Excellent written, presentation and verbal communication skills (candidate will need undergo written and oral presentation assessment) Able to work independently with minimal direction or supervision Must be comfortable managing multiple projects simultaneously in a fast-paced environment Proficient in MS Office tools such as PowerPoint, Word, and Excel Works well under pressure and meets tight deadlines Strategic and creative mindset Meticulous attention to detail

title: Marketing & Videographer Editor
description: Role: Marketing & Videographer (Filming & Editing) Location : On-site (Cafe & Roastery) Type : Full-time / Part-time / Freelance Role Overview We are looking for a creative, self-motivated individual who can independently handle social media content creation, video filming and editing, and assist with marketing and operational needs. You will play a key role in sharing our story, products, and cafe experiences through compelling video content, and maintaining a strong daily presence across our social platforms. Key Responsibilities 1. Content Creation & Video Production Film and edit engaging short-form and long-form videos featuring: Cafe food & drink preparation Coffee machines and brewing techniques Coffee beans & roasting process Behind-the-scenes of the cafe & roastery Staff highlights and customer stories Coffee culture and education content Output expectations : Short-form video per day (Reel/TikTok/FB) or long-form video every 2 days (YouTube or IGTV if applicable) Plan, create, and publish 1 post per day on Instagram, Facebook, and TikTok using content from video. Create and publish 1 blog post per week. Ensure video quality is high, brand-aligned, and optimized for platform-specific engagement. 2. Social Media Management Plan, create, and publish 1 post per day on Instagram, Facebook, and TikTok . Write engaging captions and use appropriate hashtags. Write blog posts on how to b Monitor trends, audio, and formats to keep content fresh and relevant. Manage community engagement (likes, comments, DMs) and track performance metrics. 3. Marketing Support Assist in planning and executing marketing campaigns and promotions. Contribute to brand storytelling, content calendars, and seasonal launch strategies. Help brainstorm creative ideas to grow followers and drive foot traffic. 4. Operational Support (Ad-Hoc) Support the broader team with operational tasks when needed (e.g., during busy periods or events). Help with merchandising, promotional displays, or content needs for partners. Requirements Proven experience in videography and video editing (portfolio required). Strong skills in Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or similar editing software. Experience managing brand social media accounts, particularly Instagram, Facebook, and TikTok. Ability to work independently—comfortable filming and editing solo. Strong organizational skills and ability to meet daily content deadlines. Passion for coffee and storytelling is a big plus. Flexibility to adapt to changing needs and jump in where needed across the team. Independent, accountable, punctual, motivated and a team player. Bonus Skills (Not Required but Preferred) Photography skills for still content Proficient at Social media editing. Basic graphic design (Photoshop & Premiere Pro / Video editing software). Knowledge of the coffee industry or experience in hospitality. Drone or gimbal experience for dynamic shots. MONDAY-FRIDAY ROLE. LOCATION: PANDAN LOOP & LORONG KILAT

title: G14 – UX Designer
description: Who We Are Looking For We seek a candidate who thrives in ambiguity, excels at building projects from inception, and is adept at facilitating workshops and collaborating with senior stakeholders. In addition to strong UX fundamentals, this role places emphasis on visual design quality, UI craft, and gami fi cation-informed interaction design . What you'll do Lead product design from discovery to delivery with minimal supervision Translate user needs into journeys, flows, and interfaces that drive action and adoption Plan and conduct user research, usability testing, and heuristic evaluations, and synthesise findings into actionable insights Facilitate ideation and co-creation workshops with internal teams or stakeholders Partner closely with PMs and engineers to scope and deliver solutions Present and advocate design decisions to senior stakeholders confidently Design using modern tools like Figma and Notion, and leverage AI tools to improve design speed and productivity Contribute to and evolve the design system and interaction patterns Maintain a strong feedback loop with users, analytics, and qualitative insights Balance engagement mechanics with clarity, inclusiveness, and public-sector sensibilities Requirements Has 5+ years of experience designing digital products, preferably in product-led or agile teams Can own projects end-to-end, from framing to final design handoff Strong communication skills to articulate UX, UI, and interaction decisions clearly Is confident in stakeholder engagement, including presenting to leadership/senior management Is skilled at using Figma, prototyping, and delivering responsive interfaces Has working knowledge of AI-enabled design tools (e.g., Figma Make, Loveables, Galileo AI, or Figma AI plugins) Is comfortable working in fast-paced environments and iterating quickly Is curious, empathetic, and not afraid to question assumptions Demonstrated ability to produce high-quality UI and interaction designs for production systems [Bonus] Experience creating engaging, user-centric experiences by thoughtfully incorporating game mechanics such as progression, rewards, or feedback loops is a plus.

title: Marketing Executive
description: About the role: Commerce.sg is a performance marketing and content production company. We are looking for a Marketing & Operations Executive to support our team with day-to-day marketing, project coordination and basic people management. You’ll get exposure to clients, creators and partners, and learn how a fast-moving marketing company runs. Key responsibilities: Help plan and execute marketing campaigns and content calendars (mainly TikTok & IG). Coordinate with producers, editors and designers to make sure campaigns and assets are delivered on time. Track basic performance numbers and prepare simple updates or reports. Maintain project trackers, follow up on deadlines and help improve SOPs and workflows. Support intern and junior staff management: assigning tasks, checking progress, helping with onboarding. Join meetings with clients/partners where needed and follow up on action items. Any other duties assigned by the Director to support smooth marketing and business operations. What we’re looking for Candidates with strong interest in digital marketing, social media and business operations. Hungry to learn, take ownership and grow into a bigger role over time. Comfortable with basic spreadsheets and organising information. Confident communicator, comfortable dealing with different people (internal team, interns, clients). Organised, dependable, and able to handle a fast-paced, changing environment.

title: Partner Solutions Manager
description: The Global Partnerships team manages Meta's relationships with top and emerging partners across creators, public figures, media, and entertainment. Focusing on Facebook, Instagram, Threads, and WhatsApp, the team helps drive product priorities and partner success across our family of apps. The Partner Solutions Manager will concentrate on driving product adoption, operational efficiency, and business value for Meta alongside our key Lifestyle and Wellness partners in North America. In collaboration with Strategic Partner Managers, Product, Data Analysts, and Strategy & Operations teams, this position will provide strategic planning, reporting & analysis, relationship co-management, thought leadership, product insights, and education, helping accelerate success for both Meta and our partners in the region. Responsibilities Strategic Planning: Collaborate with internal stakeholders to develop and implement comprehensive strategies and programs that drive product adoption and partner business value through Meta's family of apps Performance Analysis: Collaborate with Strategic Partner Managers to regularly analyze the performance of partner accounts/pages to identify areas of improvement and/or opportunities for product adoption Manage implementation and optimization of Meta's strategic partnerships Understand partners’ core business needs and propose innovative solutions and opportunities Reporting and Forecasting: Provide regular reports on partner performance, goal tracking, market trends and forecasting to identify potential challenges or opportunities for the in-market team. Support Product Go-To Market: Working with partners and internal teams on product launches and tests. Coordinating access, education, product feedback and measurement. Product Expertise: Share product expertise and educate Partners on our best practices & new products to help them accelerate their growth. Serve as a Product expert for internal partnerships teams and partners Cross Functional Collaboration: Act as an internal advocate on behalf of partners with cross-functional teams. Collaborate with cross functional teams and regional counterparts to build solutions at scale. Thought Leadership: Leverage data-led insights and narratives to help drive strategic decision-making. Proactively synthesize findings, develop recommendations, and communicate results as they relate to the ecosystem. Use market-specific knowledge and insights to support the execution of our business goals. Minimum qualifications 3+ years of experience in account/partner management, online operations, customer success, or equivalent 3+ years of experience working with subject matter experts, academics or at a talent agency, media, or tech company Knowledge of relationships in the talent industry and platforms for reaching audiences Experience being resourceful, proactive and detail-oriented Preferred qualifications Deep knowledge of the talent and influencer space and the evolving players in the space Experience working with cross-functional teams to develop creative partner activations Knowledge and relationships within the Lifestyle and Wellness Ecosystem Meta builds technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. description: We are an established and renowned educational publisher in Singapore since 1977. We are looking for a passionate and energetic presenter for live streaming of our products, assessment books. This position is open for full-time, project based or contract engagement. Responsibilities: • To summarize and deliver product information in text and video format. • Plan and execute engaging live streams that promote our brand and engage our audience, and achieve sales targets. • Collaborate with other team members to create compelling content for live streams • Monitor and respond to comments and questions from viewers during live streams • Able to comfortably deliver video scripts on camera for live social media streams and pre-recorded videos • Represent the brand in a professional manner during live streams • Edit new videos • Maintain and manage streaming equipment and software • Stay up-to-date with emerging trends and technologies in live-streaming • Analyse data and feedback to continuously improve the quality of our live streams • Participate in team meetings and contribute to the development of our live-streaming strategy Requirements: • Proven experience as a live streamer • Has excellent on-camera presentation skills • Strong knowledge of social media platforms and live-streaming applications • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills • Strong organizational and time-management skills • Ability to work independently and as part of a team • Flexibility to work outside of regular business hours as required • A portfolio of previous live streams or video content is highly desirable. The ideal candidate: • Passion about writing and creating interesting stories • Good time-management skills • Computer proficiency (MS Office, digital editing, web search, databases) • Familiar with social media and live streaming platforms • Able to work independently with minimum supervision • High attention to details Please send in your resume to sharonyoo@sapgrp.com Remuneration will be commensurate with qualifications and experience. Fresh graduates are welcome to apply. Please submit your resume together with a cover letter, and include any relevant portfolio, blog, recordings, or videos, if available. We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Strategic Partner Manager
description: The Global Partnerships & Content team works with partners across music, entertainment, creators, sports, community leaders and nonprofits to support the product and partnerships efforts across the family of apps. The team is focused on helping build and grow innovative and meaningful content and community engagement experiences and businesses that deliver sustainable value for our partners in the form of product adoption, fan engagement, and innovation. The team works on a wide range of product and platform initiatives, including: Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, WhatsApp, Reality Labs, and Threads. The team also provides horizontal support across these areas for deals, content strategy and planning, audience development, product marketing, creative strategy, scaled outreach, and company-wide efforts that span across industry-based, brand marketing, editorial, and product go-to-market strategies. The team is seeking a Strategic Partner Manager to initiate, execute, and scale partnerships with top creators. This role is an important part of the Creator Partnerships team, and this individual will work with both internal and external stakeholders to implement strategic partnerships plans and programs to engage established creators and ensure their success across Meta's platforms (Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, Threads, and Reality Labs). Responsibilities Combine high levels of strategic thought with execution. Adapt to changing priorities, projects and capacity of handling all elements of partner management, from maintaining high-profile relationships to supporting day-to-day operations, such as fielding and prioritizing partner requests, troubleshooting partner issues, and using analytics to improve partner performance Lead, develop, implement and track creator programs to meet the needs of the creator community and drive product adoption, retention, and innovation tied to specific company goals Collaborate with partners to identify and execute innovative partnerships across Meta's platforms. Including but not limited to marketing, editorial, and product go-to-market moments Use data and insights across to drive strategic decision-making, provide best practices and guide product adoption Identify and track industry trends, key moments, and relevant industry events to help to drive the team’s strategic approach to these opportunities Handle operational tasks, escalate product issues and feedback, and support partner troubleshooting to completion Support the broader Creator Partnerships team in helping to develop the creators directly and across various management rosters Work closely with Partnerships colleagues to coordinate on key industry opportunities (e.g. around key events, award shows, product launches, etc.) Support the creation of product education and partner best practices materials Manage time-sensitive projects through to completion while balancing evolving priorities and across a range of stakeholders Minimum qualifications BA or BS degree Experience working with creators, management, talent agencies, platform partnerships or equivalent Program Management experience Preferred qualifications In depth understanding of the digital ecosystem, social platforms and creator landscape 5+ years experience working across all levels of management, both internally and externally 5+ years experience creating strategies around complex issues, leading to thoughtful recommendations and action plans, with an added focus on using data to make decisions 2+ years experience working with and advocating for creators, talent agencies and management firms with proven success in supporting these creators' careers Demonstrated experience with prioritization and focus, as well as proven relationship building skills and effective communication Experience working across multiple teams, initiating and nurturing collaboration to achieve both team and company goals Meta builds technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. title: Performance Marketer | Maxwell | 5 Days | Up To 6K - 4461
description: Position: Performance Marketer Salary: $4000 - $6000 Working Days: Monday - Friday Working Timing: 9:30am - 6:30pm Working Location: Maxwell RESPONSIBILITIES Understand the client’s business, offer, margins, target audiences and sales process; translate that into a clear campaign plan and testing roadmap. Plan, build and manage campaigns across Google, Meta, LinkedIn and other relevant channels (TikTok/XHS/Lemon8 where suitable). Set up account structures, audiences, keywords, creatives, placements, budgets and bidding strategies; maintain strong hygiene (naming conventions, tracking, exclusions, negatives, etc.). Write and iterate ad copy (or brief writers/designers with clear direction), and run disciplined A/B testing across hooks, angles, offers, formats and landing pages. Optimise campaigns daily/weekly: reduce wastage, improve CTR/CVR, lower CPA, and scale what works without breaking efficiency. Perform keyword and audience research, and continuously expand reach through new angles, campaign types and targeting approaches. Run competitive intelligence: monitor competitor positioning, creatives, offers and funnel tactics to spot opportunities and gaps. Ensure tracking is reliable: validate conversions, events, attribution setup, UTM discipline and reporting consistency. Deliver accurate monthly performance reports with clear insights, actions taken, next experiments, and what’s needed from the client to unlock better results. Document your workflow and build SOPs with management so campaigns become easier to scale, handover and improve across the team. REQUIREMENTS Diploma or Degree in any discipline (Marketing / Business preferred). Minimum 2 years’ hands-on experience in Performance Marketing / Media Buying (agency experience is a plus). Strong working knowledge of paid media on Google Ads and Meta (Facebook/Instagram); comfortable with LinkedIn Ads. Bonus if candidate run TikTok, XHS) and/or Lemon8 campaigns. Competent with tracking and measurement tools (e.g., GA4, Tag Manager, pixels/conversion APIs, platform reporting, Looker Studio or similar). Email: supreme.jacksontan@gmail.com Tan Yong Zhi (Jackson) | Reg: R24124461 The Supreme HR Advisory Pte. Ltd | EA No: 14C7279

title: ASSISTANT IT MANAGER
description: Position Overview: As the Assistant IT Manager at Pathlab Singapore (PLSG), you will play a key supporting role in managing daily IT operations, implementing technical solutions, and maintaining essential infrastructure. This position demands strong hands-on technical skills and troubleshooting abilities, especially within healthcare IT systems. You will contribute to both strategic initiatives and day-to-day tasks under the direction of the Regional IT Manager. Additionally, you will assist in various projects and assignments as delegated, including but not limited to: Key Responsibilities: 1. IT Operations Support: Assist in managing the day-to-day operations of IT systems including the LIS, hardware, software, networks, and telecommunications. Perform first-level and second-level troubleshooting to ensure system reliability and uptime. Monitor IT systems to ensure performance, availability, and security compliance. 2. Project & Implementation Support: Provide technical and administrative support for IT projects. Work closely with internal users, vendors, and stakeholders to support project timelines and deliverables. Help track progress and provide regular status updates to the Regional IT Manager. 3. Government Liaison Support: Assist in liaising with technical representatives from Singapore government agencies (e.g., Synapse, HPB) for IT system implementation and interfacing matters. Help manage technical submissions and respond to troubleshooting issues when required. 4. Security & Compliance: Support the implementation of IT security measures and ensure enforcement of existing policies. Help prepare documentation to ensure compliance with Singapore healthcare IT regulations and audit requirements. 5. Software and Integration Support: Assist in managing and testing software development and integration projects (in-house or external). Participate in hands-on development or scripting tasks when needed. Prepare and maintain documentation related to system integrations (e.g., HL7 interface mappings). 6. User Support & Training Provide end-user training or documentation to improve adoption of new systems or updates. Serve as a point of contact for escalated IT support issues from users. 7. Continuous Improvement Recommend process or system improvements based on user feedback or performance metrics. Stay up to date with emerging technologies relevant to healthcare IT. Job Requirements: Minimum 5 years of experience supporting healthcare or lab IT systems. title: IT Trainer

many other technologies .  We
specialize in empowering both technical professionals and end users to adopt
and maximize digital tools for business success. Job Summary: We are looking for a dynamic and experienced IT Trainer to
deliver high-quality training to internal teams, partners, and end-users.  The ideal candidate will have a strong
foundation in IT systems and applications, a passion for teaching, and
preferably hold the Advanced Certificate in Learning and Performance
(ACLP). This role involves
both instructional delivery and content development across a range of
technologies, with a focus on Microsoft courses and other
relevant IT courses. Key Responsibilities: Deliver instructor-led
and virtual classroom training on Microsoft technologies (e.g., Microsoft 365,
Azure, Power Platform, Copilot, Sharepoint) and other IT system as required. Work closely with key vendors to ensure training content
is accurate, up-to-date, and aligned with business needs. Provide quality and technically relevant instruction,
assessment, and feedback in both lecture and hands-on lab environments. Prepare and maintain comprehensive course syllabus,
ensuring all learning objectives are clearly defined and met. Design, update, and maintain all instructional course
materials, including manuals, presentations, and training content. Utilize modern training technologies such as video
conferencing, distance learning platforms, and Learning Management Systems

Systems (LMS) is an advantage. Preferred Attributes: Passionate about teaching and helping others grow. Able to simplify complex technical concepts for diverse
audiences. Self-motivated, adaptable, and committed to continuous
learning. Strong organizational and time management skills.

title: Copywriter
description:
COPYWRITER A call for the concept-first, the wordsmiths, the storytellers. If you’re reading this, you’re probably looking for a place where your ideas matter, your words carry weight, and your creativity has room to grow. At BLKJ Havas, we’re looking for a Copywriter who’s ready to crack briefs open,
shape ideas into stories, and make work that people actually care about. You’ll be a voice behind the ideas — turning strategy into compelling narratives and briefs into bold creative. Working closely with art directors, strategists, and project teams, you’ll help craft campaigns that are smart, culturally relevant, and creatively sharp. You’ll write, rewrite, and push the work to be better — and in the process, sharpen your own craft. Job Responsibilities: Bring Big Ideas to Life : Contribute to the development and execution of creative concepts grounded in insight, strategy, and culture. Write Across Platforms : Craft copy for campaign platforms, scripts, social content, digital
experiences, OOH, and more — with clarity, craft, and personality. Build Brand Voice : Help shape and maintain a strong, consistent tone of voice across all touchpoints for the brands you work on. Collaborate Closely: Work hand-in-hand with art directors, strategists, and the wider creative team to ensure ideas meet both creative and client objectives. Present with Confidence : Share your work clearly and confidently with internal teams and, when required, clients. Refine the Work : Take feedback constructively and push ideas further in pursuit of stronger creative output. Stay Culture-Curious : Keep a close pulse on trends, conversations, and what’s happening in culture — bringing fresh references and thinking into the work. Grow Your Craft: Learn from senior creatives while continually developing your writing skills and creative point of view. Contribute to Creative Culture : Be an active, positive presence in a collaborative, curious, and ambitious creative environment. Requirements: 3–5 years of copywriting experience within a creative or advertising agency A strong portfolio demonstrating concept-driven thinking and solid craft in writing.
Experience working across integrated campaigns (digital, social, video, OOH, etc.). A sharp eye for language — from headlines and long-form copy to scripts and social captions. Strong communication and collaboration skills, with an openness to feedback and iteration. Ability to manage multiple briefs and deadlines without losing attention to detail. A proactive team player who enjoys working collaboratively with different disciplines. Good understanding of Singapore and regional audiences, with sensitivity to what resonates locally and across Asia. Bonus points for awards, recognitions, or work that shows your ideas stand out and travel — even better
if they’ve made people talk. About BLKJ Havas BLKJ Havas is a creative company that engineers marketing moments for brands in culture, entertainment and media. Powered by the global network of Vivendi including Lagardère, CANAL+, Prisma Media, Gameloft, and Dailymotion, we pride ourselves as the unadvertising agency. Because people hate advertising and we love people. Here at Havas, we pride ourselves on being committed to offering equal opportunities to all potential employees and have zero tolerance for discrimination. We are an equal opportunity employer and welcome applicants irrespective of age, sex, race, ethnicity, disability and other factors that have no bearing on an individual's ability to perform their job.

title: Performance Marketing Specialist (Media Buyer)
description: REQUIREMENTS Diploma or Degree in any discipline (Marketing / Business preferred). Minimum 2 years’ hands-on experience in Performance Marketing / Media Buying (agency experience is a plus). Strong working knowledge of paid media on Google Ads and Meta (Facebook/Instagram); comfortable with LinkedIn Ads. Bonus if you’ve run TikTok, 小红书 (XHS) and/or Lemon8 campaigns. Solid analytical thinking and strong written communication (you can explain performance clearly, not just show numbers). Competent with tracking and measurement tools (e.g., GA4, Tag Manager, pixels/conversion APIs, platform reporting, Looker Studio or similar). RESPONSIBILITIES Understand the client’s business, offer, margins, target audiences and sales process; translate that into a clear campaign plan and testing roadmap. Plan, build and manage campaigns across Google, Meta, LinkedIn and other relevant channels (TikTok/XHS/Lemon8 where suitable). Set up account structures, audiences, keywords, creatives, placements, budgets and bidding strategies; maintain strong hygiene (naming conventions, tracking, exclusions, negatives, etc.). Write and iterate ad copy (or brief writers/designers with clear direction), and run disciplined A/B testing across hooks, angles, offers, formats and landing pages. Optimise campaigns daily/weekly: reduce wastage, improve CTR/CVR, lower CPA, and scale what works without breaking efficiency. Perform keyword and audience research, and continuously expand reach through new angles, campaign types and targeting approaches. Run competitive intelligence: monitor competitor positioning, creatives, offers and funnel tactics to spot opportunities and gaps. Ensure tracking is reliable: validate conversions, events, attribution setup, UTM discipline and reporting consistency. Deliver accurate monthly performance reports with clear insights, actions taken, next experiments, and what’s needed from the client to unlock better results. Document your workflow and build SOPs with management so campaigns become easier to scale, handover and improve across the team.

title: Design Development Manager - Singapore
description: The Role We are seeking a Design Development Manager to grow our presence amongst Specifiers, Designers & Architects in the luxury premium space in Singapore. Fisher & Paykel has been designing products since 1934 and has grown into a global company operating in 50 countries and manufacturing in Mexico, Italy, Thailand, and New Zealand. Our design heritage is founded on a pioneering spirit and a culture of curiosity that has challenged conventional appliance design. Our goal is to be the world's number one premium appliance brand. Not only does this mean we design and build the best appliances, but that we understand who our key customers are and engage with them according to their very specific needs. The Specifier, Designer & Architect space is essential to our strategy and requires a very special skill set to develop and leverage the key relationships required. From identifying key clients, through networking and engaging with them, it's vital to our success that we've got the right person on the job. As our Design Development Manager, you will be at the forefront of cultivating relationships with top-tier specifiers, designers, and architects. Your goal will be to create strategic plans and deliver initiatives that increase product market share and achieve profitable growth. At the heart of Fisher & Paykel are our people who design innovative technologies that elevate the everyday into something out of the ordinary. We have built our business by creating moments of delight for the people who use our products. The Design Development Manager here at Fisher & Paykel Singapore is focused on creating long-lasting relationships with top-tier specifiers, designers, and architects within the country and will report directly to the SG Head of Marketing. In this role, you will work closely with specifiers and influence them to incorporate our brands into their designs. You will be a brand ambassador, promoting our portfolio of products, engaging in design discussions, facilitating product trainings, and networking within the designer/architect community. This will, in turn, drive sales through both our Builder and Retail networks. This is an individual contributor role for some who enjoys owning things end-to-end, working close to the business, and operating in a fast-moving environment. In The Role, You Will Be the subject matter expert for Fisher & Paykel within the SDA community by being curious and deeply understanding key specifier communities and their environmental influences. Initiate, develop, and attend key designer networking events within our Fisher & Paykel Singapore Showroom to strengthen existing relationships and create new opportunities for Fisher & Paykel brand, products, and services. Ensure a seamless, brand-aligned representation of Fisher & Paykel’s value proposition across all touchpoints of the key specifier community. Plan and execute the local design development strategy with key specifiers, ensuring alignment with global imperatives and growth aspirations. Provide regular reporting updates on key specifiers to the local and global Market Group. Deliver product training that is sufficiently technical but more tailored to business benefit and competitive advantage. Manage third-party agency partnerships and formulate performance and metric scorecards. Ensure the accuracy and completeness of our brand and product messaging to key specifiers. Act as a liaison for design requirements across market channels You Have Minimum of 5 years’ experience in the appliance or building industry, in a design, project management, or sales/marketing role. Marketing, Business, or Interior Design preferred in conjunction with relevant design credentials (AKBD, CKD, Leed AP). Working experience with interior/kitchen designers and architects. Proven experience in successfully developing strategic partnerships both externally and internally to deliver quality results. Strong creative & technical design skills, including the ability to read floor plans and elevations, and make recommendations on appliance selection, placement, and configuration based on spatial and proportional characteristics of the kitchen, as well as design trends and principles. Strong project planning and management experience. Excellent communication skills – verbal and written communication skills are clear and concise. Strong team player Fisher & Paykel is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, national or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability, or other characteristics protected by law.

Role Overview The Execution Trader will work closely with the Senior Execution Trader and CIO to ensure the flawless execution of trades. This is a high-impact role requiring a candidate who is dependable, calm under pressure, and capable of operating as a reliable "right hand" to the senior trading desk. Market Intelligence: Monitor real-time market developments, liquidity trends, and news flow to provide the CIO and Senior Trader with timely execution insights. Broker Relations: Maintain professional relationships with sell-side execution desks and ensure optimal service levels. Requirements Professional Experience: 1–3 years of experience in trade execution. Prior experience in a hedge fund or institutional environment is a strong advantage. You must be highly organized and able to follow strict trading protocols without constant supervision. Team Player: A collaborative attitude. You are here to support the fund's success and work harmoniously with the CIO and other functions. Technical Skills: Proficiency in Excel. Experience with EMS/OMS platforms. Communication: Strong command of English. Proficiency in Japanese is a plus given the market focus, but not a requirement.

About Ask & Embla & Ambi Who We Are: Ask & Embla is a rapidly growing jewelry brand born from alternative subcultures shaped by punk, goth, and music movements. As e-commerce brands, we ship internationally to a diverse customer base, and we are proud to be one of the leading brands worldwide in the alternative jewelry space. We’re passionate about creating a company that brings out the best in everyone, where individuals love to work and grow as a team. That means constantly learning, building new things, staying flexible about how and where we work, and empowering each other to do the proudest work of our lives. Ask & Embla and Ambi are led by an ex-Googler and ex-Amazonian, supported by dozens of team members across multiple countries. We’re building the foundation to expand into several business verticals beyond e-commerce, while continuing our operations to fulfil thousands of orders each month. Ready to Join Us? If you're a results-oriented professional who thrives in a fast-paced environment, we encourage you to apply! Learn More about Ask & Embla and Ambi: Website - https://askandembla.net/ https://ambi.ooo/ Instagram - https://www.instagram.com/askemblastore/ https://www.instagram.com/ambi.ooo/ Facebook - https://www.facebook.com/AskEmblastore/ https://www.facebook.com/ooo.ambi/ Pinterest - https://www.pinterest.com/askemblastore/ https://www.pinterest.com/ambijewelry/ TikTok - https://www.tiktok.com/@askandembla_official https://www.tiktok.com/@ambi.ooo Why Work With Us?
